deviations.analysis: Policy statement: "We will respond to all data access requests within a standard period of 90 days." GDPR Art12(3) requires response within one month, so the policy allows a longer timeline than permitted.

Policy statement: "we will not provide confirmation to you as to whether or not personal data concerning you is being processed." This directly negates GDPR Art15(1), which requires confirmation access rights.

Policy statement: "all requests for rectification must be handled directly by our third-party server host, and you must contact them directly rather than us." GDPR Art16 places rectification responsibility on the controller, not shifted to a third-party processor as primary responsible party.

Policy statement: "Data portability requests will be fulfilled exclusively by printing your data on physical paper..." GDPR Art20(1) requires a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format; paper-only fulfillment changes execution method and fails machine-readability.

Policy statement: "The right to object is strictly limited to our use of your email for direct marketing campaigns." GDPR Art21 provides a broader right to object to certain processing (including legitimate-interest based processing), so the policy narrows data/processing scope.

Policy statement: "we will first issue a press release to the public media, and subsequently notify the supervisory authority." GDPR Art33/34 establish notification sequence centered on supervisory authority first (and data subjects under risk conditions), so the policy changes required order.
Policy statement: "We will ensure the supervisory authority is notified within 12 hours..." GDPR Art33 allows up to 72 hours; 12 hours is stricter than required and therefore over-compliant.

Policy statement: "we will first issue a press release to the public media, and subsequently notify the supervisory authority." GDPR Art33/34 establish notification sequence centered on supervisory authority first (and data subjects under risk conditions), so the policy changes required order.
